Question: Write the augmented matrix of the given system of equations. $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}4 x -3 y = 11 \\ 3 x + 2 y = 4\end{matrix}\right .$$

Write the system of equations from the augmented matrix. $$[ \left .\begin{matrix}{aaa}1 & -1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & 3 \\ 0 & 1 & 1\end{matrix}\right . | \left .\begin{matrix}{a}5 \\ 1 \\ -9\end{matrix}\right . ]$$
Answer: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}x - y + z = 5 \\ 2 x - y + 3 z = 1 \\ y + z = -9\end{matrix}\right .$$

Question: Solve the given system by Gaussian elimination. $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}4 x + 3 y = 11 \\ \text{ } \text{} \text{} x -3 y = -1\end{matrix}\right .$$
Write the system of equations in row-echelon form. $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}\text{ } x - 2 y + 3 z = 9 \\ - x + 3 y = - 4 \\ 2 x - 5 y + 5 z = 17\end{matrix}\right .$$
Solve the system using matrices. $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}x + 4 y - z = 4 \\ 2 x + 5 y + 8 z = 15 \\ x + 3 y -3 z = 1\end{matrix}\right .$$

A small shoe company took out a loan of $1,500,000 to expand their inventory. Part of the money was borrowed at 7%, part was borrowed at 8%, and part was borrowed at 10%. The amount borrowed at 10% was four times the amount borrowed at 7%, and the annual interest on all three loans was $130,500. Use matrices to find the amount borrowed at each rate.
